changes.reason: Batch D profile-only admission study. Prior duplicate guard rejected the exact 0.5/1/25 geometry, and the failed run_039 variant added an ES-VWAP cap. This uses the grid midpoint 1.5 to require stronger actual breakout follow-through than the current 1.0 without touching exits or global vetoes.

Raise higher-low confirmation from 0.5 to 1.0 so any admitted charm-wall breakaway has support beneath it. This is the support leg of the charm-wall thesis and avoids repeating broad VWAP-distance pruning.

Keep the successful broader charm-wall distance box concept available at 25, but pair it with stronger ES structure rather than a tighter TP or VWAP cap. This tests whether reachability can improve without admitting weak wall-chase entries.

expected_effect: This should test charm_wall_breakaway_long reachability on the run_029 leader base while preserving convex payoff behavior. Desired result is added or restored charm-wall trades with no slow >=12h trades, profile PnL/exposure near or above 225, total net profit within 98% of run_029, and global worst MAE no worse than -513.4. Main tradeoff is that the stronger higher-high/higher-low pair may remain too selective and produce a flat run.
label: promote
rationale: Use Batch D as one logical strategy-profile family because the best-by-score base is run_029, latest run_043 is a rejected indicator-timing pathology, and broad filters from run_043 retain too little PnL. This is not the duplicate run_033 keyset and does not repeat the failed charm-wall exit compression or ES-VWAP cap. It probes the strategist's charm-wall reachability hypothesis with admission geometry only, preserving option-confirmed routing and avoiding missing/stale 0DTE optimization.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: run_043 shows the bad tail regime is concentrated in pullback/charm-pin path behavior: two slow trades consumed 116.1 exposure hours, while protected fast winners overlap broad static features. Static filters such as gamma score >=0.5 retained only 84.1% PnL and call/put imbalance >=0.5 retained 77.6%.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Early Progress Heat Classifier
strategy_hypotheses.risk: Can overfit a handful of delayed recoveries or block clean extended-VWAP winners unless protected fast-winner UTC retention is explicitly validated.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: VALID_0DTE pullback_long or charm_pin_pullback_long entries that fail to make meaningful positive progress before MAE in the first few minutes.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: Remove slow >=12h trades, reduce worst MAE toward leader-family levels, retain >=90% pullback/charm-pin PnL, retain >=95% protected fast winners, and keep total score flat or better versus run_029.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: VALID_1DTE_ONLY remains efficient in run_043 with 7 trades, $464.5 PnL, 145.99 PnL/exposure hour, no slow trades, and -327.25 worst MAE, while prior direct 1DTE tightening hurt score and net profit.
strategy_hypotheses.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Split
strategy_hypotheses.risk: Router overlap may cannibalize profitable breakout_long_1dte trades or the companion profile may be unreachable with current triggers.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: 0DTE unavailable, 1DTE map valid, and ES state resembles pullback/reclaim rather than clean breakout continuation.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL stays at or above current 1DTE baseline, 1DTE average/worst MAE improves, breakout_long_1dte retained PnL is measured, and total net profit remains >=98% of leader.
